declaration.code: func saveLivePhoto(to output: PHContentEditingOutput, options: [String : Any]? = nil, completionHandler handler: @escaping (Bool, (any Error)?) -> Void)

overview:
## Discussion

Use this method when you have finished an editing session and need to provide rendered output in a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput] object. Unlike when rendering output for a photo or video asset, you don’t need to provide rendered output using the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput/renderedContentURL] property of the editing output object. Instead, create a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput] object using the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput/init(contentEditingInput:)] initializer, passing the same [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object you used in the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHLivePhotoFrameProcessingBlock] initializer to start this Live Photo editing context. Then pass that editing output object to this method, and Photos renders the Live Photo and provides it to the editing output.

After this method’s completion handler signals successful rendering, you use the content editing output to complete the edit. In an app using the Photos framework, create a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAssetChangeRequest] object inside a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHPhotoLibrary] `performChanges` block, and set its [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAssetChangeRequest/contentEditingOutput] property to your editing output. In a photo editing extension running in the Photos app, your main view controller provides content editing output when requested by the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/PhotosUI/PHContentEditingController/finishContentEditing(completionHandler:)] method.

# saveLivePhoto(to:options:completionHandler:)

**Instance Method**

Processes and saves a full-quality Live Photo as the output of your editing session.

## Declaration

```swift
func saveLivePhoto(to output: PHContentEditingOutput, options: [String : Any]? = nil, completionHandler handler: @escaping (Bool, (any Error)?) -> Void)
```

```swift
func saveLivePhoto(to output: PHContentEditingOutput, options: [String : Any]? = nil) async throws
```

## Parameters

- **output**: The photo editing output to receive the rendered Live Photo, created from the same [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object you used to begin this Live Photo editing context.
- **options**: Options that affect Live Photo rendering. See `Live Photo Processing Options`.
- **handler**: A block that Photos calls on the main queue after rendering is complete. The block takes the following parameters:



## Discussion

Use this method when you have finished an editing session and need to provide rendered output in a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput] object. Unlike when rendering output for a photo or video asset, you don’t need to provide rendered output using the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput/renderedContentURL] property of the editing output object. Instead, create a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput] object using the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingOutput/init(contentEditingInput:)] initializer, passing the same [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object you used in the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHLivePhotoFrameProcessingBlock] initializer to start this Live Photo editing context. Then pass that editing output object to this method, and Photos renders the Live Photo and provides it to the editing output.



After this method’s completion handler signals successful rendering, you use the content editing output to complete the edit. In an app using the Photos framework, create a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAssetChangeRequest] object inside a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHPhotoLibrary] `performChanges` block, and set its [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAssetChangeRequest/contentEditingOutput] property to your editing output. In a photo editing extension running in the Photos app, your main view controller provides content editing output when requested by the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/PhotosUI/PHContentEditingController/finishContentEditing(completionHandler:)] method.

## Processing an Editing Context’s Live Photo

- **prepareLivePhotoForPlayback(withTargetSize:options:completionHandler:)**: Processes a Live Photo with your edits for viewing.
- **PHLivePhotoEditingOption**: Keys for the `options` dictionary used with the methods listed in Processing an Editing Context’s Live Photo.
- **cancel()**: Aborts any Live Photo processing in progress.
